post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 154: The First Cop Mafia!


If you are dead or are not in this game, please do not post in this thread.


Please read all the rules as this game has several major differences from typical newbie games.

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
Everybody thought we would continue to prosper. After all, what reason was there to steal or kill? The conscience is strong. And the lure of wealth gained through evil deeds would not be enough to convince people to put down their moral standards and sin against their neighbors, no?

But it was.

And now in our little town, there are criminals. Cruel, vicious, and full of self-interest. No consideration for others.
No regard for what is right and wrong. Stopping at nothing to grab more wealth for themselves.

But we have hope. One of us, far ahead of our league, has the genius to deduce whether one is a dastardly murderer and thief. With all of our strength combined, we can hang these criminals and live in peace once again.

But we must be both careful and quick. Our mayor and banker have been murdered already, leaving only nine. Two of these remaining are the threat to everything we have. We must eliminate them quickly.

ShineBunny has died Night 0. He was the Mayor (Moderator).
Kagebaka has died Night 0. He was the Banker (Co-Moderator).


General Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or co-moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes small font size, white-colored font, and others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, do so before submitting your post into the thread.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
  • Do not attempt to abuse or exploit any of the rules.


Voting/Lynches
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell and capitalize the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames. Do not attempt to abuse this rule.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Votes are locked during LyLo. This means that if the number of mafia alive is exactly one less than the number of townspeople alive during the day, any votes made are final and cannot be unvoted.
  • If at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • The Mafia team will have a QuickTopic thread which they can communicate in at any time.


Activity/Day/Night
  • Each day phase will last 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours have elapsed since the start of day.
  • Each night phase will last for 48 hours.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day. Do not post until the moderator makes the post announcing the end of night, even if the deadline for night passes. To show you have read all the rules, make a post in your Role PM that states your role and flavorname.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • Each player must make at least 15 contentful posts per day phase. Failure to do so will result in a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • If any player commits an excessive amount of rule infractions, they will be force-replaced. If the infraction committed is severe enough and impacts the game to an extent where force-replacing would not solve the problem, such as a player posting a screenshot of their Role PM in thread, I will be forced to modkill that player. Please do not make me modkill you as it affects the game adversely.


Moderators
  • I (ShineBunny) am the Moderator of Game 154. Kagebaka is the co-moderator of Game 154.
  • Red-colored text (#f00) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#00f) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#38761D) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This game will use the Cop 9'er setup. This is an open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. 2 of them are Mafia Goons. One of them is a Town Cop. The Town Cop gets an investigation pre-game (Night 0) on a random townie. Essentially, the Town Cop has one player shown to them to be confirmed town before the start of Day 1.


Role PMs
The First Cop (Town Cop) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are The First Cop (Town Cop). Unparalleled in wit and cleverness, you alone have the ability to deduce the true nature of the remaining townspeople. Every night, you can go out to investigate clues you have found during the day to pinpoint one as an honest citizen or a worthless criminal.

Every night, you may tell me the name of one player who you wish to investigate. You will be notified at the start of the next day of that player's alignment as Town or Mafia. Additionally, you know that [player] is a townie. The link to the game thread is [link].

You win when all the Criminals (Mafia) are dead.
Citizen (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are a Citizen (Vanilla Townie). In your little town, you strive to cooperate with your fellow citizens to create a comfortable, healthy lifestyle for all. But there are criminals among you who wish only to gain wealth for themselves. Your strength to defeat them lies in your voice and your vote. The link to the game thread is [link].

You win when all the Criminals (Mafia) are dead.
Criminal (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],
You are a Criminal (Mafia Goon). The foolish dreams of unity of the townspeople do not interest you. You wish only to take the wealth and comfort of others for yourself, no matter the cost. With your fellow criminal [player], you aim to kill the townspeople off and take their possessions for yourself.

Each night, you and your partner share a factional kill. You can communicate with your partner at any time in this QuickTopic. The link to the game thread is [link].

You win when the number of Criminals (Mafia) alive is greater than or equal to the number of townspeople alive, or when nothing can prevent the same.


Alive (2)
  1. pandabear10 Nike100 summitwei (IC)
  2. frogshot Charge


Dead (7)
  1. MathLegend27, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was strung up on the gallows on Day 1.
  2. jeffisepic, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was stabbed to death on Night 1.
  3. hello120471, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was mobbed to death on Day 2.
  4. math_turtle37, The First Cop (Town Cop), was hanged on Night 2.
  5. SFGiantsFan, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was pranked to death on Day 3.
  6. AppleYum, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was endgamed on Day 3.
  7. e_is_cool, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was endgamed on Day 3.


Significant Events
Charge replaces frogshot.
Nike100 replaces pandabear10.
MathLegend27, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was lynched Day 1.
jeffisepic, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was killed Night 1.
hello120471, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was lynched Day 2.
summitwei replaces Nike100.
math_turtle37, The First Cop (Town Cop), was killed Night 2.
SFGiantsFan, Citizen (Vanilla Townie), was lynched Day 3.


Warnings
MathLegend27 (1), for editing post #45.
Charge (1), for OOT.
hello120471 (1), for a personal attack in #211
AppleYum (1), for borderline gamethrowing.



Do not post until this line is crossed out.

You may now post. Day 1 begins now and ends at 2:37 PM PST on December 21.

post_rendered:
Hello.
Anyways a tl;dr for people who didn't bother to read the rules:
Setup: 6 VT, 1 Cop, 2 Goons; Cop gets a N0 check on town.
Deadline lynch is always active; majority lynch is enabled starting from Day 2.
Votes are locked at LyLo. Please don't instantly vote someone during LyLo unless you're a Cop and have an innocent on the other person or a guilty on the person you are voting.

Anyways I just wanted to make sure that everyone gets these rules that are different from normal. (Why are there so many experimental newbie setups right now?)

Obligatory thing about saying how I'm an IC or whatever but here's something super important: Please don't claim as cop super early on. Don't fakeclaim as cop either. Just don't claim unless you have a result on everyone who is alive except one; then you may claim. There's probably some other situations to claim in like getting a guilty on both mafia but other than that it's generally a bad idea to claim early.

I would post that super giant post about why you should never no lynch, but the gist of it is that lynches are kills controlled by town; after all, there is a town majority. Also, lynching every day allows the "better" townies to make it to LyLo, even in the worst case scenario.

post_rendered:
Hi e_is_cool

this strategy is standard and proven really effective in cop 9, or cop anything. I've played in many many cop MU games, and we utilized this strategy in all of them. We can continue to debate this (scum have narrower POE etc.) but overall the whole point of this game is for the cop to out their peeks without actually playing. That takes town cooperation, and this is one of the best ways to do it.

Another rule of thumb is that wolves will be on the constant lookout for seerhunting. Commiting to a n0 read means you will not back down this read at all costs. This is essential. The longer the cop is alive, the better. Treat this person like your highest townread the whole game. Better off not even mentioning your person until the cop is dead.

Seer hunt clear is when a Vanilla Townie dies during the night. That person's N0 would be cleared. Assuming optimal play, the mafia will always go for the cop. A VT kill implies that the mafia thinks that this person was in the seer POE based off of their peeks.

My n0 is e_is_cool

post_rendered:
I see many people are voting for me, which is why I expected would happen. I have, after all, posted a lot of suspicious things. But i have a question for all of you: Why would I make such vote-inducing posts?
Well, the answer is simple: I don't really care about this game. It is, after all, my first game, and I'm not a mafia. No mafia would risk being lynched by posting a lot on the first day. Also, nobody who cared would post so much when there's not a lot of information to go off of.
Of course, I could be a mafia posting this to get suspicion off me. That, however, is a horrible idea. No matter what argument I give, there's still going to be more people suspecting me then if I did nothing in the first place.
So, vote for me if you want to, but try finding someone who isn't so self-destructively impulsive in the future.

Story and flip
Having determined the previous day to avenge the deaths of our partners, we gathered our council together. We met near the newly constructed gallows. They loomed ominously over us while we discussed who would meet their end this day. Knowing that inefficiency and lack of rigor would mean our doom, we spent as much time as we could deliberating among ourselves. There were several small clues that seemed to indicate the alignment of some of those among us, which we used as leads to spark discussion.

Most of us were actively analyzing the evidence we had, but soon it became apparent that MathLegend27 and AppleYum, though trying to appear helpful, were not actually putting in real effort towards solving the mystery of who the murderers were, instead putting up a forced show of enthusiasm and making distracting comments which detracted from our true cause. Having noticed this odd behavior, we argued among ourselves which of these two was a criminal or simply a lazy citizen.

Though there was not a large amount of concrete evidence, our conclusion was that MathLegend27 was a criminal in disguise, based on his behavior today. As the sun went down, we grabbed MathLegend27 and strung him up on the gallows. However, when we took down his lifeless body and investigated what he possessed on him and in his house, we determined that he could not possibly be a criminal and that the real threats were still lurking among us. We were greatly saddened by this, but with the darkness approaching, we hurried home to our houses to sleep in anxiety, each one of us hoping that our life would not be the next one taken by the murderers.

MathLegend27 was strung up on the gallows Day 1. He was a Citizen (Vanilla Townie).

post_rendered:
Story and flip
The criminals were harder to catch than we expected as evidenced by our failure. MathLegend27's death put fear into our hearts. Would we be picked off one by one by the heartless robbers among us?

These thoughts lurked within our minds as we hurried to our once safe homes the night before. Where once there had been peace and happiness, now was filled only with a sick dread of being the next victim to lose their life and have their possessions plundered. Despite the measures we took to fortify our houses, we were doubtful if they would deter the criminals from carrying out their evil deeds.

We woke up the next day and gathered together beside a fine view on a cliff. But nature's vista was not in our minds and we took no thought to the beauty surrounding us, only filled with apprehension. Our fears concerning the security of our houses were confirmed as we soon noticed that jeffisepic was not with us.

We went to investigate his house and found what we expected. The door, cleverly unlocked from the outside by some fiendish scheme, creaked ominously as we opened it. The criminals did not bother to hide anything. Inside, we found smashed plates and cups and furniture in disarray. And among it all lay jeffisepic's body, stabbed a thousand times over, a clear display of the malice of the greedy traitors among us.

jeffisepic was stabbed to death Night 1. He was a Citizen (Vanilla Townie).

jeffisepic wrote:
Hi e_is_cool

this strategy is standard and proven really effective in cop 9, or cop anything. I've played in many many cop MU games, and we utilized this strategy in all of them. We can continue to debate this (scum have narrower POE etc.) but overall the whole point of this game is for the cop to out their peeks without actually playing. That takes town cooperation, and this is one of the best ways to do it.

Another rule of thumb is that wolves will be on the constant lookout for seerhunting. Commiting to a n0 read means you will not back down this read at all costs. This is essential. The longer the cop is alive, the better. Treat this person like your highest townread the whole game. Better off not even mentioning your person until the cop is dead.

Seer hunt clear is when a Vanilla Townie dies during the night. That person's N0 would be cleared. Assuming optimal play, the mafia will always go for the cop. A VT kill implies that the mafia thinks that this person was in the seer POE based off of their peeks.

Story and flip
Shaken from the deaths of MathLegend27 and jeffisepic, we resolved to be less hasty over choosing who to dispose of. As we gathered on the hill outlooking the beautiful view of the sun shining down onto the grassy plains, we put all of our detective skills to the test. We told ourselves that we would avenge the deaths of our fallen brethren.

But it was not to be. Despite all our efforts, we failed once again. Of course, it didn't help at all that two or three of us were sleeping through much of the discussion, rendering them completely unhelpful. But whatever the excuse we could make up, no matter how legitimate it was, it could not change the fact that the one we branded as a criminal and fairly mobbed to death out of rage was an innocent citizen as well, honestly striving to help us eliminate the criminals but falling by the hands of his own fellow citizens in the quest to deal justice to the murderers.

The sun was red that day, shining down on hello120471's unlucky corpse as if to display all the bloodshed that divided us all apart. There was not much time left; we had the eliminate the murderers among us, and more quickly than ever.

Story and flip
Covered in the sun's red light, we hurried home. Covered in blood, I thought. Defeat seemed inevitable. Was there really any hope left? It seemed that the criminals were too far ahead of us, staying hidden while we only seemed to be helping them kill off the remainder of the honest citizens left. Did we really have another chance?

One more. Just one more. But a very slim one, and that was because that night the one person who could truly tell the alignment of us was murdered. We found math_turtle37's body strung up on a noose on a hook in his room. Luckily for us, math_turtle37 left some clues that seemed to indicate the alignment of e_is_cool. But we couldn't be completely sure. The criminals seemed to be in control, being able to track math_turtle37 down and kill him.

As we mourned math_turtle37's death, we vowed to avenge his and the other three citizens' deaths. But this was our final chance, and one more failure would mean death. We had to find the criminals and deal them justice, and now.

This game actually was an unfortunate circumstance on the cop covering strategy, and I don't want others to think that this strategy is bad by any means:

-both wolves were good players. They were both subbed at one point meaning they got to avoid attention for a long time.
-there were way above expected cop checks on an actual wolf. By day 1 there were like 2 possible cops.
-the cop check was the same as the seer hunt clear, and the cop indirectly outed himself D1
-jeffisepic was the n0

Honestly, even with all these downsides, we went to LyLo with 1 confirmed town, and 1 near-confirmed wolf (charge)

post_rendered:
jeffisepic wrote:
-there were way above expected cop checks on an actual wolf. By day 1 there were like 2 possible cops.

As for this point, this is why you shouldn't immediately drop a random peek (on any day). Of course the odds are that you pick a town for your random peek, but it isn't mandatory to drop a peek at the beginning of the day. By analyzing people, you can townhunt and pick a player you're confident to be town of as your peek, making it more likely for you to pick a townie as your fake peek and thus reducing the chance of eliminating yourself as a possible cop (I believe this was covered in the MU articles I linked as well, just putting it here as well).

-there were way above expected cop checks on an actual wolf. By day 1 there were like 2 possible cops.

As for this point, this is why you shouldn't immediately drop a random peek (on any day). Of course the odds are that you pick a town for your random peek, but it isn't mandatory to drop a peek at the beginning of the day. By analyzing people, you can townhunt and pick a player you're confident to be town of as your peek, making it more likely for you to pick a townie as your fake peek and thus reducing the chance of eliminating yourself as a possible cop (I believe this was covered in the MU articles I linked as well, just putting it here as well).

I agree with this - personally I don't drop peeks at all but rather hint towards them
This way also allows me to control my cop equity better
